atoms.semantic_claim: The downstream diff for crates/transaction-pool/src/lib.rs introduces behavior or wiring that must be accounted for in the v2.2 merge target.

destinations.snippet:
fn submit_async_result(&self, submission: crate::pool::AsyncResultSubmission) -> Result<(), crate::pool::AsyncPoolError>;
fn get_async_metadata(&self, original_tx_hash: &TxHash) -> Option<crate::pool::AsyncTxMetadata<Self::Transaction>>;
destinations.evidence: The v2.2 public trait still exposes async lifecycle result submission, metadata lookup, fulfilled-original replay, and delivery-ready replay boundaries needed by RPC and payload building.
Pool wrappers forward the async lifecycle APIs to PoolInner for consumers that hold the concrete Pool type.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The behaviorally important old lib.rs txpool additions are covered by current async lifecycle methods and sender-lock admission rules. The old dedicated scheduled and async-commitment subpool insertion methods are not restored because scheduled execution is built from parent state and async commitments enter lifecycle tracking only after canonicalization.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Submit async result metadata through the pool trait and assert payload-facing fulfilled-original lookup returns only non-expired fulfilled entries; attempt a new sender transaction while async metadata locks that sender and expect SenderLocked.
atoms.notes: The old diff also added Ritual tx lifecycle metrics and direct scheduled/async subpool APIs. Current behavior keeps the async RPC/result and lifecycle APIs, but does not restore the removed metrics crate or dormant scheduled-pool submission path.

summary: Atoms in this file have destination evidence or exact coverage after the feature-ordered pass.
